notes:
A small follow-up to 0.6.4's **Sync Deep Audit** release, closing the last few gaps where a synced change needed a second sync to land.

**Fixes & polish**

- **Notes from another device finish applying in the same sync.** Changes pulled from a second device used to sometimes wait for the *next* sync to actually appear; they now drain and apply within the same sync pass, so what you see after a sync is the whole of what arrived. (PR #569; Sync Deep Audit 2026-07-17 follow-up.)
- **Blocks that only had checkbox or reorder changes now sync their content too.** The last missing-content gap: a block whose only recorded history was ticking a checkbox or being reordered could sync those changes without its actual text — its content now travels with it. (Sync Deep Audit 2026-07-17 follow-up.)
- **Signing in on a new device says "Downloading your notes…".** The first sync after signing in now shows that your notes are on the way, instead of appearing stuck on "Connecting…" while the initial download runs. (PR #569.)

**Under the hood**

- The parked-op drain now runs as a **bounded fixpoint after the final pull batch**, so ops whose prerequisites arrive late in the same sync still apply before the cycle ends. (PR #569; Sync Deep Audit 2026-07-17 follow-up.)
- The genesis-backfill predicate is now **keyed on missing upsert ops** rather than op count, so blocks with only checkbox/reorder history are correctly backfilled with their content. (Sync Deep Audit 2026-07-17 follow-up.)
- Block-meta ops for a block that is **absent locally now park** (awaiting the block) instead of silently no-oping. (Sync Deep Audit 2026-07-17 follow-up.)
